data_out.description: Structured inventory of standalone web applications with live URLs and publication links

domain_knowledge: The domainMASSTs project comprises six complementary web applications (microbeMASST, plantMASST, tissueMASST, microbiomeMASST, foodMASST, metadataMASST) developed in the Dorrestein Lab at UC San Diego for domain-specific mass spectrometry library searching.
Each standalone web application is independently deployed and accessible via a live URL; these applications allow single-spectrum queries against curated spectral libraries.
metadataMASST serves an aggregation and visualization role for multi-application search outputs, distinct from the domain-specific query tools.
The authoritative source code repository is hosted on GitHub under GNPS_MASST (github.com/mwang87/GNPS_MASST) and archived on Zenodo (DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.492844724).
README documentation in the repository should explicitly list each application's deployed URL and any associated peer-reviewed publications describing methods or validation.

direct_checks: verify file README.md exists in github.com/robinschmid/microbe_masst repository
verify file README.md exists in the domainMASSTs repository (github.com/mwang87/GNPS_MASST or equivalent root repo)

extract from README.md a structured table or list containing columns: [application_name, live_url, publication_link]; row_count_equals 6 (one per tool: microbeMASST, plantMASST, tissueMASST, microbiomeMASST, foodMASST, metadataMASST)
field_present: each row must have non-empty application_name matching one of the six known tool names
field_present: each row must have live_url field with a valid URL format (http:// or https://)
field_present: each row must have publication_link field (may be empty if tool is not yet published, but field structure must be present)
verify that all six application names appear exactly once in the extracted table — no duplicates, no missing entries
robust to whitespace/formatting variations in README rendering across git platforms
expert_review: verify that each live_url actually resolves and serves the advertised web application (human or automated browser check required)
verify that each publication_link (where provided) is a valid and active DOI, PubMed ID, or persistent URL
confirm that the table/list structure in README.md is the canonical, current, and complete inventory of domainMASSTs tools — no tools listed elsewhere in README should be missing

task_description: Extract and verify the complete list of standalone web applications (microbeMASST, plantMASST, tissueMASST, microbiomeMASST, foodMASST, metadataMASST) from the domainMASSTs repository README, documenting their live URLs and associated publication links.
task_objective: Compile and verify the registry of all six domain-specific MASST web applications with their canonical URLs and linked publication DOIs.

task_outputs.label: Structured inventory of standalone web applications with URLs and publication links

title: Reproduce the enumeration of domain-specific MASST components and their published URLs from the repository README
uncertainty_notes: This card was generated by the LLM-assisted pipeline and needs scientific expert review.
Each TracedClaim's evidence_span has been substring-checked against its source section; see logs/llm_calls.jsonl and capsules/<task_id>/quality_report.json for groundedness results.

Synthesis grounding: the following tools/outputs were NOT found in the source paper and are inferred — verify before use: plantMASST, tissueMASST, microbiomeMASST, foodMASST, metadataMASST, Structured inventory of standalone web applications with live URLs and publication links.
workflow_description: 1. Clone or retrieve the domainMASSTs repository from the Zenodo deposit (10.5281/zenodo.492844724) or GitHub source (github.com/mwang87/GNPS_MASST). 2. Locate and parse the README file to identify all documented standalone web applications and their metadata. 3. Extract the live URL for each application (microbeMASST, plantMASST, tissueMASST, microbiomeMASST, foodMASST, metadataMASST) and any associated peer-reviewed publication links. 4. Validate that each URL is accessible and responds correctly. 5. Compile extracted data into a structured inventory file (CSV or JSON) with columns for application name, live URL, publication DOI/link, and verification status.
